using System;
using System.Collections.Concurrent;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

/// <summary>
/// Provides thread-safe optimization utilities for numerical arrays.
/// </summary>
public static class Optimizer
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Validates the input array, performs bounded parallel evaluation of each element,
    /// and returns the best (maximum) score found.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="input">The array of double values to evaluate. Must not be null or empty.</param>
    /// <returns>The highest score computed from the input elements.</returns>
    /// <exception cref="ArgumentNullException">Thrown when <paramref name="input"/> is null.</exception>
    /// <exception cref="ArgumentException">Thrown when <paramref name="input"/> is empty.</exception>
    /// <remarks>
    /// Complexity: O(n) time where n is the length of <paramref name="input"/>, with bounded parallelism
    /// to avoid resource exhaustion. Space complexity is O(1) beyond the input array.
    /// </remarks>
    public static double Optimize(double[] input)
    {
        if (input == null)
            throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(input));
        if (input.Length == 0)
            throw new ArgumentException("Input array must not be empty.", nameof(input));

        // Bounded parallel evaluation using ParallelOptions to limit concurrency
        var options = new System.Threading.Tasks.ParallelOptions
        {
            MaxDegreeOfParallelism = Environment.ProcessorCount
        };

        double bestScore = double.NegativeInfinity;
        var lockObj = new object();

        Parallel.ForEach(input, options, value =>
        {
            double score = Evaluate(value);
            lock (lockObj)
            {
                if (score > bestScore)
                {
                    bestScore = score;
                }
            }
        });

        return bestScore;
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Evaluates a single input value and returns a score.
    /// This is a placeholder implementation that can be replaced with actual business logic.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="value">The input value to evaluate.</param>
    /// <returns>A computed score for the given value.</returns>
    private static double Evaluate(double value)
    {
        // Example scoring function: replace with actual optimization logic
        return Math.Sin(value) * Math.Cos(value) + value * 0.1;
    }
}
